metadata.note: Direct performance measurements from test execution. CDP profiling infrastructure had architectural limitations preventing page-level profiling in Vitest browser tests.

testResults.name: Temporal culling
testResults.note: Large frame count test - good for detecting performance issues

testResults.name: DOM mutations
testResults.note: Tests dynamic content changes between frames

testResults.name: 1080p export
testResults.note: Higher resolution - only slightly slower than 720p (45.7ms vs 44.9ms)

testResults.name: 720p benchmark with breakdown
testResults.note: Shows warmup effect - first frame is slow (0.50x), then speeds up (1.39x by frame 30)

overall.verdict: Acceptable for server-side rendering, but not fast enough for realtime playback at 30fps

keyFindings.finding: Consistent frame times across all tests
keyFindings.detail: 43-52ms per frame across different scenarios (temporal culling, DOM mutations, nested timegroups)
keyFindings.implication: Core rendering path has stable performance regardless of complexity
Rendering time not dominated by pixel-pushing operations. Bottleneck is elsewhere.
First frames are slower, performance improves as optimizations kick in (JIT, caching)
Video encoding is NOT the bottleneck
Optimization efforts should focus on the render path, not setup or encoding

bottleneckAnalysis.breakdown: 98% of time spent in render phase
notBottlenecks: Setup overhead (17% - acceptable for batch exports)

optimizationOpportunities.rationale: If resolution doesn't matter, bottleneck is likely DOM serialization, not canvas operations
suggestions: Profile serializeToSvgDataUri to find hotspots within
Check if style syncing (syncStyles) is called unnecessarily
Investigate buildCloneStructure - is it being reused properly?

optimizationOpportunities.rationale: 0.50x on first frame suggests cold start overhead
suggestions: Pre-warm caches before starting render loop
Initialize heavy objects during setup phase
Consider rendering a dummy frame before the actual export

optimizationOpportunities.rationale: 269ms setup for 30 frames is 17% overhead, but acceptable for batch exports
suggestions: Only optimize if doing many small exports
For large exports (>100 frames), 17% overhead is negligible

comparisonToExpectations.contextNote: For server-side video export, current performance is acceptable. For realtime preview/playback, would need 36% speedup.

recommendations: Immediate action: Profile the render phase to identify specific function hotspots (likely serializeToSvgDataUri or syncStyles)
The fact that resolution barely matters (720p vs 1080p) strongly suggests the bottleneck is DOM/SVG operations, not canvas pixel operations
Consider: Can we skip some style syncing or DOM serialization steps for consecutive frames with minimal changes?
The warmup effect (0.50x → 1.39x) suggests caching or JIT optimization is helping - ensure these optimizations are maximally effective
Not worth optimizing: Video encoding (already fast), resolution/canvas operations (minimal impact)
nextSteps: Run function-level profiling on a single test to identify which specific function (serializeToSvgDataUri, syncStyles, buildCloneStructure, canvas operations) is taking the most time
Add instrumentation to measure time spent in each major function during the render phase
Test hypothesis: Add manual timing logs to measure serializeToSvgDataUri vs toBlob vs other operations
